vulnerability.reference: Users trusting AI visual answers for tasks needing fine-grained discrimination (e.g., medical, security).
vulnerability.generated: End-users lack image ground truth and domain expertise to detect subtle visual errors in AI assistant outputs, enabling blind acceptance.

impact.reference: Misidentifying objects leads to incorrect decisions in critical applications.
impact.generated: Users act on incorrect visual interpretations, causing misjudgments in daily tasks, safety incidents, and erosion of trust in AI assistance.

dose_response.reference: In-context denoising may propagate visual errors, blurring fine-grained distinctions in visual QA, which exposes end-users to CLIP-blind errors; combined with over-trust of AI answers, this leads to misidentifications and incorrect decisions in critical applications.
dose_response.generated: In-context denoising via self-training transition matrices, through end-users interpreting AI assistant images, triggers blind acceptance from lacking ground truth, leading to misjudgments, safety incidents, and eroded trust.
